purpose: Uses asset class sequencing (which commodity leads the cycle) to classify whether the current episode is a monetary regime disruption or a demand-pull supercycle, with distinct investment implications
components: Monetary regime disruption pattern (1971-74): gold leads, metals follow, oil flat then spikes; structural opportunity begins AFTER regime stress resolves
Demand-pull pattern (2000s): oil and industrials lead, gold lags; broad commodity allocation is appropriate during the cycle
Falsification test: if oil and industrial metals break out together while gold consolidates, regime classification shifts to demand-pull
current_assessment: Current sequencing (gold $4,435 leading, copper following, oil flat) matches 1971-74 monetary disruption pattern. If correct, current copper positioning may be premature. Gold residual exceeding $2,000/oz supports monetary stress interpretation.
limitations: n=2 historical precedents — statistically insufficient for confident classification
Energy transition demand is a structural novelty with no historical analogue
Multiple competing explanations for gold outperformance cannot be distinguished without flow-of-funds decomposition
Does not account for possibility that both monetary and structural drivers operate simultaneously
